## Review

_Congratulations, you've completed this exercise and learned the fundamentals of HTML!_

<img src="https://octodex.github.com/images/jetpacktocat.png" alt="celebrate" width=200 align=right>

Here's a recap of your accomplishments:

- You understand what HTML is and how it structures web pages
- You can recognize and explain common HTML tags and their purposes
- You can read and interpret HTML code
- You understand semantic HTML and why accessibility matters
- You can work effectively with AI to generate and understand HTML code
- You created a personal portfolio webpage using HTML

### What's next?

- **HTML Forms**: Learn how to collect user input with form elements
- **HTML Tables and Media**: Learn how to organize data and embed media content
- **Web Accessibility**: Dive deeper into building accessible web experiences
- **HTML Documentation**: [MDN Web Docs - HTML Basics](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Learn/Getting_started_with_the_web/HTML_basics)
- **Semantic HTML in Practice**: Keep improving page structure with meaningful tags

### Tips for Your Learning Journey

- **Practice regularly**: Build small projects to reinforce what you've learned
- **Use AI as a learning tool**: Ask AI to explain concepts and generate code examples
- **Read other people's HTML**: View the source code of websites you admire (right-click → "View Page Source")
- **Experiment boldly**: HTML is forgiving—try new tags and see what happens
- **Don't memorize syntax**: Instead, focus on understanding concepts and using AI to help with specific syntax

Remember: Modern developers don't memorize everything—they understand concepts and collaborate with tools (including AI) to build great things. You're already on that path!
